channel.title: Home on Hotblog: The Perfect Hotdog Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Hotblog: The Perfect Hotdog Blog

item.title: The Journey Thus Far
item.description: The hotdog that made me realize my life’s purpose was made with my friend William on the Lunar New Year, Tuesday, February 17th. That evening we had tentative plans with a friend of mine to grab food in celebration. Me and william ended up getting hungry far before that and decided, with limited options, that hotdogs would be our best bet. Me and William had been making hotdogs on and off for the past five years or so, sometimes over summer at a bbq, sometimes at my house over a school lunch break. We had invented what we called “Liz and William Dogs”, a dog sliced lengthwise to be cooked like a patty. We’d serve that up on some toasted bread with some American cheese. We also invented something called “Omledog” but I include that among the few true failures of my lifetime. The Tuesday in question was a simple sort of dog. Grilled quarter pound beef franks on a toasted potato roll, sweet relish and horseradish mustard. Like a part time lover, a pair of serviceable dogs left both me and William satisfied but wanting more. This feeling sparked in me the core realization of the hotdog project - it was time to begin in earnest. I spent the rest of the night babbling to anyone who would listen, going on about toppings and study locations and whatever other nonsense I came up with that evening.
